title: Thinking, Feelings, and Imagination Game Zone
source: Generated from approved unit runtime and linked master dictionary

games.title: Meaning Match
games.description: Match each unit word to its approved child-friendly meaning.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: A thought or plan that comes to you in your own mind.
choices: idea
imagine
reason
rounds.answer: idea
rounds.explanation: idea: A thought or plan that comes to you in your own mind.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: A feeling inside you that helps you notice or understand something.
choices: idea
imagine
sense
rounds.answer: sense
rounds.explanation: sense: A feeling inside you that helps you notice or understand something.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: Feeling that nothing will get better, however hard you keep trying.
choices: imagine
hopeless
idea
rounds.answer: hopeless
rounds.explanation: hopeless: Feeling that nothing will get better, however hard you keep trying.

games.title: Spelling Builder
games.description: Choose letter tiles to build important unit words.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: To make a picture of something inside your mind before it happens.
rounds.answer: imagine

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: The heavy feeling you carry inside when something makes you unhappy.
rounds.answer: sadness

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: So pleasant that you feel happy while you are doing it.
rounds.answer: enjoyable

games.title: Sentence Puzzle
games.description: Put unit words into the correct sentence order.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: I have an idea for a fun game we can play.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: I have a reason for being late; my bus was slow.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: She felt sadness when her friend moved away.

games.title: Language Choice
games.description: Choose the form that makes the unit language accurate.

rounds.prompt: In The Box of Ideas, Amal says: “That story has a strong ______.” Which word completes her line?
choices: purpose
sense
reason
choice
rounds.answer: reason
rounds.explanation: Amal is talking about why the writer once felt hopeless, and the word for that cause is reason.

rounds.prompt: Sami said, “I can imagine flying.” Which sentence reports his words correctly?
choices: Sami said I can imagine flying.
Sami said he can imagine flying.
Sami says that he could imagine flying.
Sami said he could imagine flying.
rounds.answer: Sami said he could imagine flying.
rounds.explanation: Reported speech moves I to he and can to could, while the reporting verb said stays in the past.

rounds.prompt: In Feelings Are Not Bad or Good, why does the writer compare sadness with rain?
choices: To show that sadness is a feeling, just as rain is a kind of weather.
To warn readers that a rainy day will always make a child feel sad and lonely.
To explain how clouds and puddles are made in the sky.
To show that sadness lasts exactly as long as a rain shower.
rounds.answer: To show that sadness is a feeling, just as rain is a kind of weather.
rounds.explanation: The text calls rain a kind of weather, so the comparison makes sadness ordinary rather than bad.

games.title: Reading Detective
games.description: Use unit evidence to solve each reading clue.

rounds.prompt: What did Teacher Yasmin bring into class, and what did she ask each child to add?
choices: She brought a bright box called the Box of Ideas, and each child adds one idea to it.
The writer compares sadness with rain, showing that sadness is only one kind of feeling.
Because she is reporting what Sami said rather than speaking as Sami herself.
rounds.answer: She brought a bright box called the Box of Ideas, and each child adds one idea to it.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: She brought a bright box called the Box of Ideas, and each child adds one idea to it.

rounds.prompt: What did Leo suggest for the class garden, and what reason did he give?
choices: The writer compares sadness with rain, showing that sadness is only one kind of feeling.
He still felt unsure, and the story says he looked around but did not smile.
Leo suggested growing tomatoes, because the class can eat them at lunch.
rounds.answer: Leo suggested growing tomatoes, because the class can eat them at lunch.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: Leo suggested growing tomatoes, because the class can eat them at lunch.

rounds.prompt: How did Sami feel after he folded his paper, and which detail shows it?
choices: The paper about a father who was ill, because the writer shared a real and painful memory.
He still felt unsure, and the story says he looked around but did not smile.
Nora felt hopeless because her cat was lost, and her mother listened to her.
rounds.answer: He still felt unsure, and the story says he looked around but did not smile.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: He still felt unsure, and the story says he looked around but did not smile.

games.title: Speaking Quest
games.description: Listen, record, review and submit useful unit language.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 1 — Share an Idea
rounds.target: I have an idea. I suggest we ______.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 3 — Imagine It!
rounds.target: Imagine you can ______ (fly / talk to animals / be invisible).

rounds.prompt: Speaking 5 — Explain and Respond
rounds.target: Explain why sharing sadness can help a friend.

games.title: Word Order Race
games.description: Arrange unit language in a clear, accurate order.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: She gave me a sincere smile when I helped her.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: Playing games with friends is very enjoyable.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: I suggest we play outside after lunch.

games.title: Definition Dash
games.description: Choose the approved meaning before moving to the next clue.

rounds.prompt: What does 'sadness' mean in this unit?
choices: The heavy feeling you carry inside when something makes you unhappy.
A thought or plan that comes to you in your own mind.
To make a picture of something inside your mind before it happens.
rounds.answer: The heavy feeling you carry inside when something makes you unhappy.
rounds.explanation: sadness: The heavy feeling you carry inside when something makes you unhappy.

rounds.prompt: What does 'allow' mean in this unit?
choices: A thought or plan that comes to you in your own mind.
To make a picture of something inside your mind before it happens.
To let somebody do something, or to give them your permission.
rounds.answer: To let somebody do something, or to give them your permission.
rounds.explanation: allow: To let somebody do something, or to give them your permission.

rounds.prompt: What does 'imagine' mean in this unit?
choices: The cause that explains why something happens or why you did it.
To make a picture of something inside your mind before it happens.
A thought or plan that comes to you in your own mind.
rounds.answer: To make a picture of something inside your mind before it happens.
rounds.explanation: imagine: To make a picture of something inside your mind before it happens.

games.title: Word Type Power
games.description: Identify how each vocabulary word works in English.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'thoughtful'?
choices: Adjective
Noun
Verb
rounds.answer: Adjective
rounds.explanation: thoughtful is used as a adjective in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'enjoy'?
choices: Noun
Adjective
Verb
rounds.answer: Verb
rounds.explanation: enjoy is used as a verb in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'choice'?
choices: Adjective
Noun
Verb
rounds.answer: Noun
rounds.explanation: choice is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

games.title: Memory Pairs
games.description: Reveal tiles and connect each word with its meaning.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 1.
pairs: The cause that explains why something happens or why you did it.
The job that something is made for, or why a person does it.
A feeling inside you that helps you notice or understand something.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 2.
pairs: Something that happened to you and stays inside your memory.
Kind, because you stop and think about what other people need.
Feeling that nothing will get better, however hard you keep trying.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 3.
pairs: To let somebody do something, or to give them your permission.
To like doing something so much that you want more of it.
A thought or plan that comes to you in your own mind.

games.title: Question Quest
games.description: Read each unit question carefully and choose the best answer.

rounds.prompt: Nora gave a sincere apology. What does sincere tell us about her?
choices: She spoke quickly and then walked away.
She said sorry in a very loud voice.
She truly meant the words she said.
She wrote her apology instead of saying it.
rounds.answer: She truly meant the words she said.
rounds.explanation: Sincere means honest and truly meaning what you say, so her apology came from the heart.

rounds.prompt: In A Group Discussion, Teacher Yasmin says: “Please ______ an idea, and allow everyone to speak.” Which word fits?
choices: believe
suggest
remember
enjoy
rounds.answer: suggest
rounds.explanation: She is asking the class to offer ideas, and the verb for offering an idea is suggest.

rounds.prompt: Which sentence uses its modal verb correctly?
choices: He cans swim across the pool.
She will swims to the island.
They musts listen to the teacher.
We should help the new student.
rounds.answer: We should help the new student.
rounds.explanation: A modal verb never adds -s, and the verb after it stays plain, so should help is the correct pair.

games.title: Unit Mission
games.description: Finish with a mixed challenge across the whole unit.

rounds.prompt: Which meaning belongs to the word purpose?
choices: The job that something is made for, or why a person does it.
A dark shape that appears when something blocks the sunlight.
The decision you make when you pick one thing from two or more.
Something that happened to you and stays inside your memory.
rounds.answer: The job that something is made for, or why a person does it.
rounds.explanation: Sami does not know the purpose of his idea, which means he cannot say what job it is meant to do.

rounds.prompt: In The Box of Ideas at School, which word completes this line: “Imagine something ______,” I told him?
choices: quieter
better
louder
later
rounds.answer: better
rounds.explanation: The narrator encourages Sami to picture a stronger idea, so the missing word is better.

rounds.prompt: Which one of these words is an abstract noun?
choices: sadness
playground
teacher
umbrella
rounds.answer: sadness
rounds.explanation: You cannot see or touch sadness, while a playground, a teacher and an umbrella can all be touched.
